task: Audit whether existing AI_architecture features are truly necessary; classify remove/improve/keep candidates using Codex plus subagent debate

content: Initial audit claim: no core deletion should happen yet. Keep agent-flow, quality-gate, resume-readiness, feature tiers, reference/adoption/release manifest, generated-artifact policy, and plan_id evidence as core skeleton invariants. Treat v2 specialist runtime, experimental adapters, LSP/knowledge/source-recovery, domain skills, and duplicated validators as candidates for demotion, merge, or trigger-narrowing rather than immediate deletion.

Removal skeptic critique: v2 specialist runtime and experimental adapter placeholders are weakly justified by real usage and make the skeleton look like a separate runtime. Prefer freezing or optional packaging; demote LSP, knowledge-gap, domain active skills, and source-recovery if usage evidence stays weak.
evidence: Harvey subagent: v2 specialist runtime ranked #1 cleanup candidate; adapter placeholders ranked #2; LSP/knowledge/domain skills/source-recovery ranked as demote or optional candidates

content: Maintenance/verifier critique: deletion is too strong, but duplicated validation surfaces and unused lifecycle ledgers increase operational cost. Improve validator boundaries, merge skill-surface-check with surface-bloat-audit, demote empty usage/lifecycle ledgers to optional until real capture exists, and align SKELETON_UPGRADE canonical/generated docs with manifest.yaml.
evidence: Ampere subagent: quality-gate/verify-skeleton duplicate calls; skill-surface-check overlaps surface-bloat-audit; skill usage and lifecycle ledgers have zero records; generated/canonical docs drift exists

content: Preservation architect critique: core agent-flow, validation gates, feature tiers, release/upgrade machinery, surface-bloat audit, copied-source ledger, and source-anchor strict option preserve the skeleton role. Do not delete them. Instead narrow subagent/specialist/delegation triggers, shrink Notion root-rule exposure, and separate stable core from stable advisory tools.
evidence: Euler subagent: no immediate core deletion; preserve feature tiers and release machinery; reduce root visibility of optional/incubating features

forbidden_actions: delete or demote files without a separate approved implementation plan
implementation_scope.summary: Read-only feature necessity audit converged: no immediate core deletion; propose a future cleanup slice that (1) freezes/optionalizes v2 specialist runtime instead of deleting it, (2) removes experimental adapter placeholders from feature-status while preserving ADR, (3) separates stable core from stable advisory tools, (4) merges or clarifies overlapping skill-surface and surface-bloat audits, (5) narrows root Notion/subagent/delegation exposure, and (6) aligns generated/canonical upgrade docs with manifest.yaml.
validation: dialogue-lint passes; subagent audit consensus captured
